title: Pedro H. A. Anjos
text:
Multiphase Flows • Environmental Flows • Interfacial Dynamics





I develop computational models and numerical simulations to investigate multiphase flows, transport phenomena, and evolving fluid interfaces in environmental and engineering systems.
My research combines fluid mechanics, applied mathematics, and scientific computing to study porous media flows, interfacial instabilities, gravity currents, sediment transport, and particle-laden environmental flows.










Transport processes involving miscible and immiscible fluids, porous media flows, and particle-laden systems. My work combines numerical simulations and theoretical modeling to understand complex transport phenomena across a broad range of environmental and engineering applications.









Numerical investigations of turbidity currents, gravity currents, sediment transport, and particle-laden environmental flows. These systems play a central role in natural hazards, submarine sediment transport, and environmental mixing processes.









Hydrodynamic instabilities, viscous fingering, magnetic fluid interfaces, and nonlinear pattern formation. This research explores how fluid interfaces evolve under competing physical mechanisms and external forcing.

text:
Transport processes involving miscible and immiscible fluids, porous media flows, and particle-laden systems. My work combines numerical simulations and theoretical modeling to understand complex transport phenomena across a broad range of environmental and engineering applications.









Numerical investigations of turbidity currents, gravity currents, sediment transport, and particle-laden environmental flows. These systems play a central role in natural hazards, submarine sediment transport, and environmental mixing processes.

title: Contact
text:
Visiting Researcher
Department of Mechanical Engineering
Pontifical Catholic University of Rio de Janeiro (PUC-Rio, Brazil)
I am always interested in discussing research collaborations, faculty positions, teaching opportunities, and interdisciplinary projects in fluid mechanics, soft matter physics, and scientific computing.











pamorimanjos@esp.puc-rio.br




Google Scholar
ORCID
LinkedIn


Feel free to contact me regarding research collaborations, academic opportunities, or graduate student supervision.
